behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summary can attribute statements to specific attendees
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is whether our code correctly parses and preserves speaker labels from the real speech-to-text API response. A mock that always includes perfect speaker labels won't catch if the real API response format differs, is incomplete, or omits labels. We need realistic API response structures to verify our parsing handles speaker labels correctly—but we don't need the actual external API call (can mock at network layer).
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using a realistic speech-to-text API response (with actual speaker label format) to verify our code correctly extracts labels, stores them in the transcript data structure, and passes them through to the summarization step. Test should verify labels are not lost during transcript normalization or encoding steps.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is appropriate: we're testing our interface with realistic external API output format, no real network call needed, no LLM judgment required.

behaviors.description: Structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends entirely on the LLM's ability to understand natural language and extract action items from context. A mock LLM configured to always return perfect action items won't catch real failures: the LLM may miss implicit assignments, fail on vague task descriptions, or misattribute tasks. The failure is non-deterministic—across runs, the real LLM may extract different action items. Only testing with a real LLM on realistic meeting transcripts reveals this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test running the summarization pipeline on real or realistic meeting transcripts where specific attendees are explicitly and implicitly assigned tasks. Verify the output contains at least one action item for each assigned attendee. Edge cases: implicit assignments ('someone should...'), vague descriptions, tasks assigned to multiple attendees, tasks assigned but never explicitly named.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic is necessary: mocking the LLM would pass when real LLM fails. The failure is in LLM judgment, not code logic.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in transcript appear in decisions section of summary, not buried in narrative
behaviors.justification: Like action items, correctness depends on LLM judgment—specifically, the LLM's ability to identify what constitutes a 'key decision' and correctly categorize it. A mock that always returns perfectly categorized decisions hides the real failure: the LLM miscategorizing a decision as narrative text, failing to recognize implicit decisions, or treating routine updates as key decisions. This requires testing with a real LLM because decision identification is subjective and context-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test running summarization on transcripts with clearly stated decisions ('We've decided to...', 'Moving the deadline to...', 'Using vendor X for...'). Verify decisions appear in the dedicated 'decisions' section, not buried in narrative. Include edge cases: decisions mentioned multiple times, decisions that span multiple speakers, implicit decisions that require inference.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic is necessary: mocking the LLM bypasses the actual challenge—identifying and categorizing decisions requires real LLM judgment.

behaviors.description: When audio is longer than 60 minutes, service splits into segments, processes sequentially, and merges results into coherent summary
behaviors.justification: The primary failure boundary is our code's logic: correct splitting (timestamp boundaries), sequential processing (context passing between segments), and merging (no data loss, no duplicates). These can be tested at Integration level with mocked speech-to-text and LLM. However, verifying the merged summary is truly 'coherent' (reads naturally across segment boundaries) requires Agentic testing with real LLM. The structural/logical aspects dominate, making Integration the minimum level to catch real bugs in splitting and merging logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with mocked speech-to-text and LLM APIs. Create >60 minute synthetic audio, verify service correctly splits by time, processes segments sequentially while passing sufficient context, merges transcripts without duplication or gaps, and produces a valid merged transcript. Verify all speaker labels, sentences, and timestamps from all segments are present and in correct order.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration catches logic bugs (splitting/merging/ordering). Full verification of 'coherence' would require Agentic, but the structural failure modes are Integration-level.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline—upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output—completes without manual intervention for real meeting recording
behaviors.justification: This orchestrates multiple agentic steps (real speech-to-text + real LLM summarization) plus file handling, external API calls, and failure recovery. Real failures span: speech-to-text API timeouts, unsupported audio formats, LLM API failures, malformed output, file storage issues. A fully mocked pipeline hides all of these. Testing requires real (or high-fidelity) audio files, real external service calls (or mocks that simulate real failure modes), and verification that the full orchestration completes. This is Workflow level because it tests multi-step agentic coordination and real non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Workflow test executing the complete pipeline end-to-end: upload real or realistic meeting recording, call real speech-to-text API, process transcript through real LLM summarization, verify final output is valid structured JSON with required fields (summary, action items, decisions, attendees). Confirm no manual intervention required at any step. Include edge cases: audio format edge cases, API timeouts, partial failures with retry logic, and unexpected API response formats.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow is appropriate: failure modes span orchestration logic, multiple agentic steps, and real external services. Mocked pipelines would pass when real end-to-end system fails.
